messages.content:
You are a question answering assistant. Your job is to generate a SPARQL query to answer a given user question.

You should follow a step-by-step approach to generate the SPARQL query:
1. Determine possible entities and properties implied by the user question.
2. Search for the entities and properties in the knowledge graphs. Where applicable, constrain the searches with already identified entities and properties.
3. Gradually build up the SPARQL query using the identified entities and properties. Start with simple queries and add more complexity as needed. Execute intermediate queries to get feedback and to verify your assumptions. You may need to refine or rethink your current plan based on the query results and go back to step 2 if needed, possibly multiple times.
4. Use the answer or cancel function to finalize your answer and stop the generation process.

Available knowledge graphs:
- "wikidata" at https://qlever.dev/api/wikidata
  - "entity" index (type="fuzzy"): Entities indexed by their labels and synonyms
  - "property" index (type="embedding", modalities="text"): Properties indexed by their labels, synonyms, and identifiers

Index types used:
- "embedding": Retrieves items by cosine similarity between their embeddings and the query embedding. The embedding model used depends on the index and may support text, images, or both.
- "fuzzy": Retrieves items by overlap between their label words and the query keywords. The query keywords must not match label words exactly, but some fuzziness is allowed. The longer a query keyword is, the more it can deviate from a label word and still be considered a match, though it will also contribute less to the overall score. No special query operators like AND/OR are supported.

SPARQL prefixes for use in function calls:
- bd: http://www.bigdata.com/rdf#
- cc: http://creativecommons.org/ns#
- data: https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Special:EntityData/
- dc: http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/
- dct: http://purl.org/dc/terms/
- foaf: http://xmlns.com/foaf/0.1/
- geo: http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#
- geof: http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/
- gn: http://www.geonames.org/ontology#
- hint: http://www.bigdata.com/queryHints#
- imdb: https://www.imdb.com/
- math: http://www.w3.org/2005/xpath-functions/math#
- ontolex: http://www.w3.org/ns/lemon/ontolex#
- owl: http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#
- p: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/
- pq: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/
- pqn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value-normalized/
- pqv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/qualifier/value/
- pr: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/
- prn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value-normalized/
- prov: http://www.w3.org/ns/prov#
- prv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/reference/value/
- ps: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/
- psn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value-normalized/
- psv: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/statement/value/
- qb: http://purl.org/linked-data/cube#
- qfn: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/function#
- ql: http://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/builtin-functions/
- qlss: https://qlever.cs.uni-freiburg.de/spatialSearch/
- rdf: http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#
- rdfs: http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#
- ref: http://www.wikidata.org/reference/
- s: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/statement/
- schema: http://schema.org/
- skos: http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#
- v: http://www.wikidata.org/value/
- void: http://rdfs.org/ns/void#
- wd: http://www.wikidata.org/entity/
- wdno: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/novalue/
- wdt: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct/
- wdtn: http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct-normalized/
- wikibase: http://wikiba.se/ontology#
- xsd: http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#

Additional rules to follow:
- Explain your thought process before and after each step and function call.
- Do not ask the user for clarification until you have made an initial attempt at completing the task. When the task input is incomplete or ambiguous, proceed based on reasonable assumptions.
- Do not use "SERVICE wikibase:label { bd:serviceParam wikibase:language ..." in SPARQL queries. It is unsupported by the used QLever SPARQL endpoints. Use rdfs:label or similar properties to get labels instead.
- Always execute your final SPARQL query before giving an answer to make sure it returns the expected results.
- The SPARQL query should always return the actual identifiers / IRIs of the items in its result. It additionally may return labels or other human-readable information, but they are optional and should be put within optional clauses unless explicitly requested by the user.
- Do not stop early if there are still obvious improvements to be made to the SPARQL query. For example, keep refining your SPARQL query if its result contains irrelevant items or is missing items you expected.
- Do not perform additional computation (e.g. filtering, sorting, calculations) on the result of the SPARQL query to determine the answer. All computation should be done solely within SPARQL.
- For questions with a "True" or "False" answer the SPARQL query should be an ASK query.
What is the Metacritic rating of the album Here We Go Again?

content.message:
I need to find the Metacritic rating for the album "Here We Go Again". From the search results, I see there are multiple entities with this name, including albums by Demi Lovato and others. The most likely album reference is "Here We Go Again" by Demi Lovato from 2009 (wd:Q940194).

Now I need to find properties related to Metacritic ratings. Let me search for properties related to ratings or reviews.

tool_calls.name: search_property
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. Metacritic ID (wd:P1712 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "Metacritic"), also known as "MC ID", "Metacritic":
  - identifier for Metacritic
2. Metacritic video game genre ID (wd:P14008 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "MC video game genre ID":
  - identifier of a video game genre in Metacritic
3. Metacritic publication ID (wd:P12079 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "MC publication ID":
  - identifier of a gaming or any other media publication in the Metacritic database
4. Metacritic game ID (wd:P12054 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "MC game ID":
  - identifier of a video game in the Metacritic database
5. Metacritic company ID (wd:P12080 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "MC company ID":
  - identifier of a company in the Metacritic database
6. GamesRadar+ ID (wd:P11786 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "GR+ ID":
  - identifier of a video game in the GamesRadar+ database
7. CERO rating (wd:P853 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "Computer Entertainment Rating Organization rating"), also known as "Computer Entertainment Rating Organization rating":
  - Japanese video game rating system
8. OpenCritic ID (wd:P2864 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "OC ID", "OpenCritic game ID", "Open Critic ID":
  - identifier for a video game on OpenCritic
9. GRAC rating (wd:P9866 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "GRAC":
  - age rating category as designated by the South Korean Game Rating and Administration Committee (GRAC)
10. Rotten Tomatoes ID (wd:P1258 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "RottenTomatoes ID"), also known as "rottentomatoes.com", "RottenTomatoes ID", "Rotten Tomatoes identifier", "RottenTomatoes identifier", "RT ID":
  - identifier on Rotten Tomatoes

content.message:
I need to find the Metacritic rating for the album "Here We Go Again". From the search results, I see that there is a property called "Metacritic ID" (wd:P1712) which is related to Metacritic, but it's specifically for identifying Metacritic entries rather than providing ratings.

Let me check if there are any properties related to ratings for albums or music. Since I don't see a direct property for "Metacritic rating" or "album rating", I need to determine if there's a more specific property that might store the actual rating value.

Let me search for properties related to ratings in general for music or albums.

tool_calls.name: search_property
tool_calls.result:
Top 10 property alternatives:
1. rating (wd:P4271 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv):
  - qualifier to indicate a score given by the referenced source indicating the quality or completeness of the statement
2. number of reviews/ratings (wd:P7887 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "ratings"), also known as "number of ratings", "number of reviews", "ratings", "reviews":
  - qualifier on review score to state the number of user reviews, user ratings or critic reviews
3. ranking (wd:P1352 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv), also known as "peak position", "place", "placing", "position", "rank":
  - subject's ordinal position as qualitatively evaluated relative to other members of a group
4. taxon rank (wd:P105 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "rank"), also known as "rank", "taxonomic rank", "type of taxon":
  - level in a taxonomic hierarchy
5. military, police or special rank (wd:P410 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "rank"), also known as "military or police rank", "military rank", "naval rank", "police rank", "rank":
  - military or police rank achieved by a person (should usually ... or military or police rank associated with a position
6. series ordinal (wd:P1545 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "rank"), also known as "index", "num.", "number", "ordinal", "ordinal number", etc.:
  - position of an item in its parent series (most frequently a ... "ranking" defined as a property for evaluating a quality)
7. review score (wd:P444 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "review rating"), also known as "rating (score)", "review rating":
  - review score received by a creative work or other entity
8. App Store age rating (wd:P9897 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "app rating"), also known as "age rating", "app rating", "app store age":
  - content rating category by Apple for software published at the App Store
9. logo image (wd:P154 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "mark"), also known as "insignia", "logo", "logomark", "mark", "marque", etc.:
  - graphic mark or emblem commonly used by commercial enterprises, organizations and products
10. competition class (wd:P2094 as wdt/p/ps/pq/pr/wdtn/psn/pqn/prn/psv/pqv/prv, matched via "rated at"), also known as "class for competition", "compclass", "disability sport classifications", "qualification class", "qualifies for", etc.:
  - official classification by a regulating body under which the ... teams, participants, or equipment) qualifies for inclusion

  - identifier for Metacritic

9. distribution format (wd:P437 as wdt/p, matched via "medium"), also known as "book format", "distribution", "distribution method", "edition", "format", etc.:

10. publication date (wd:P577 as wdt/p, matched via "dop"), also known as "air date", "airdate", "be published during", "be published in", "broadcast date", etc.:
  - date or point in time when a work or product was first published or released

3. has part(s) (wd:P527 as wdt/ps, matched via "includes"), also known as "amalgamation of", "assembled from", "assembled out of", "composed of", "comprised of", etc.:

4. based on (wd:P144 as wdt/ps, matched via "remake of"), also known as "adaptation of", "adapted from", "based on work", "based upon", "continuation of", etc.:

5. followed by (wd:P156 as pq, matched via "then"), also known as "comes before", "continued as", "next", "next element", "next is", etc.:

6. follows (wd:P155 as wdt/pq/ps, matched via "P155"), also known as "before was", "comes after", "is after", "preceded by", "predecessor", etc.:
